description: A cloud of tiny water droplets suspended near the ground, lighter than fog. 'Wheiluro' names the ethereal veil—moisture suspended between rain and air, softening edges, creating mystery, touching skin with invisible wet.
sound_symbolism: The word drifts in suspension. 'Whe' /ʍe/ begins with whispered moisture, breath becoming visible. The vowel hiatus 'e.i' suspends in air, neither falling nor rising. Then 'lu' holds liquid form, and 'ro' drifts onward, the mist moving through.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo wheiluro' (the mist). Takes the elemental classifier 'themo' when counted. Distinguished from 'nuphari' (fog) by lightness and visibility. Associated with mystery, dawn, and gentle obscuring.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Mist creates the soft-focus beauty beloved by Art Nouveau—edges dissolved, forms suggested rather than declared, mystery in the visible.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Mist teaches the insubstantial nature of perception—what seems solid dissolves on approach, what obscures also reveals.
